description_long: This guard stone features a robust, vertically elongated base with a rough, stone-like texture and a weathered, mottled brown coloration. The base is adorned with a single, arched blue panel on each visible side, possibly representing a decorative or reflective element. Atop the base, a large, faceted blue crystal or stone is suspended, capped at both ends with small spherical nodes, giving the object a striking, ornamental appearance. The surface of the base is irregular and appears to mimic natural rock, with deep grooves and protrusions, while the blue crystal above is sharply angular and semi-translucent. The overall design suggests a blend of functional urban barrier and decorative monument, likely intended to prevent vehicle encroachment while serving as a visual landmark. The object exudes a sense of durability and permanence, suitable for placement in high-traffic or historically themed urban environments.
description: A rugged, stone-textured guard stone with arched blue panels and a large, faceted blue crystal suspended above, blending functional barrier and decorative monument.
description_view_0: Front view shows the arched blue panel centered on the rough stone base, with the blue crystal directly above.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the same arched blue panel but with slightly different stone surface details and the crystal aligned above.
description_view_2: Rear view displays another arched blue panel, with the stone texture and crystal consistent with other sides.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the arched blue panel and the irregular stone surface, with the crystal above.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the corners of urban streets, near building entrances, or at the edges of pedestrian zones to prevent vehicle intrusion and mark boundaries.
attribute_installation_orientation: Fully above ground, upright, with the base resting on pavement or stone and the crystal element elevated above.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing on the lower stone base from vehicle contact and foot traffic, with possible discoloration or erosion from weather exposure. The blue panels may exhibit fading or surface scratches, while the crystal could show minor surface dulling from environmental factors.
